channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Rudraṭa knights tour - GSS Murthy
item.description: The Knight’s Tour Problem and Rudraṭa’s Verse: A View of the Indian Facet of the Knight’s Tour By G S S Murthy [Affiliation: FIETE; Corresponding author: G S S Murthy Email:murthygss gmail] Abstract If a chess-knight is moved on a vacant chess-board [8x8 square] such that it visits each one of the 64 squares once and once only, the knight is said to execute a Knight’s Tour. Solution to the knight’s tour problem was known in India as early as the 9th century AD as a demonstration of wizardry in composing 32-syllable verses in Sanskrit.
